seoTitle: Crawford River vs Crawney — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Crawford River and Crawney. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Crawford River vs Crawney: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Crawford River and Crawney.
heading: Crawford River vs Crawney — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Crawford River, New South Wales and Crawney, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Crawney has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Crawford River (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Crawford River or Crawney safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Crawford River has a flood risk score of 29/100, while Crawney scores 28/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Crawford River scores 46/100 for bushfire risk. Crawney scores 29/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Crawford River or Crawney?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Crawney has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.95% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
